title: duguetia calycina
intro: Duguetia calycina, also known as the Brazilian cherry or mururé, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest in South America, belonging to the Annonaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its bark, seeds, and leaves have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Duguetia calycina include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
